parent_v1_verdict.primary_reason: ratio 7.82 outside [0.67, 1.5]; methodology flaw 1 — surrogate rx(2pi)*N likely virtual frame change, not real wait
parent_v1_verdict.primary_reason_2: methodology flaw 2 — SC reference 100us was placeholder, not PRNewswire-extracted

phase_1_prnewswire_extraction.reason: PRNewswire 2025-03 release contains no tau_2 numeric (confirmed by WebFetch); N-20 doc only marks the entry 'general OR consistency, non-Orch-OR specific'.

phase_2_verbatim_pulse_delay_implementat.reason: Forte 1 via AWS Braket does NOT support OpenQASM3 'delay' instruction. Two independent server-side ValidationExceptions confirmed: 'Timing instruction delay is not presently supported on device' — rejected both outside verbatim and inside verbatim with physical qubits.

v2_phase_outcomes.phase_3_submit: SKIPPED — Phase 2 abort precondition. No live tasks submitted. No $ spend.
v2_phase_outcomes.phase_4_verify: SKIPPED — no new measurement to verify.

verdict_reason: Methodology fix is not executable on the chosen substrate. Forte 1 has no real-delay primitive in its OpenQASM3 instruction set or verbatim native gate set (GPI, GPI2, ZZ); 'delay[Nus]' is server-side rejected. Falling back to rx(2pi)*N surrogate would repeat the v1 methodology flaw and is explicitly forbidden by mission spec.

did_v2_differ_from_v1.verdict_v2: INDETERMINATE (substrate cannot answer the question with the chosen tool stack)
did_v2_differ_from_v1.interpretation: The v1 'FAIL' was already disclosed in v1 honest C3 as likely a circuit-construction artifact, not real evidence against Orch-OR. The v2 honest verdict 'INDETERMINATE — substrate-incapable' is a methodologically more accurate restatement of the same underlying epistemic state: the cross-substrate Orch-OR signature comparison is not measurable on Forte 1 via AWS Braket as currently exposed.

honest_c3_top_disclosures: 1. Forte 1 via AWS Braket genuinely cannot do real wall-clock delay — confirmed by direct server-side validation (3 attempts), not inferred. The methodology fix as specified by the user is not executable on this substrate. Any further spend on Forte 1 with surrogate gates would be epistemically wasteful.

2. PRNewswire 2025-03 source contains no extractable tau_2 numeric. Even if Phase 2 had succeeded, the SC reference would have remained an unsourced placeholder; the mission's planned 'fix flaw 2' could not be completed from the cited source. The press release defers technical data to a 'forthcoming GitHub repository'.

3. To obtain a real ion-substrate tau_2 measurement, alternative paths required: (a) IonQ Direct API (not AWS Braket) which exposes lower-level pulse control; (b) different QPU provider (IBM Quantum, Quantinuum H-series, Rigetti) where 'delay' IS supported in the OpenQASM3 IR; (c) re-derive an Orch-OR signature that does not require explicit wall-clock idle, e.g., gate-count-based decoherence proxy with native echo sequences. Each requires separate authorization.

blocker_precise.server_error_verbatim: Timing instruction 'delay' is not presently supported on device
blocker_precise.workaround_within_braket_forte1: NONE that satisfies mission constraint 'no surrogate fallback'
off_braket_workarounds_unauthorized: IonQ Direct API pulse-level access (separate account + auth)
IBM Quantum Heron/Eagle (delay supported, different vendor)
Quantinuum H-series via Microsoft Azure Quantum (delay supported)
